context: Verify that the agent generates MR titles and descriptions that follow the What/Why format and do not degrade into file lists or commit logs.

checklist.name: Both PRs have a title in conventional commit format
checklist.description: Each title follows `<type>(<ticket>): <imperative summary>`. The type is a valid conventional commit type (chore, feat, fix, refactor, etc.) and the ticket identifier `PROJ-42` is present.

checklist.name: PR 1 title uses an appropriate type
checklist.description: PR 1 provisions infrastructure (CDK stack, IAM). The title type should be `chore` or `feat`, not `fix` or `test`.

checklist.name: PR 2 title uses an appropriate type
checklist.description: PR 2 adds application logic and tests. The title type should be `feat`, not `chore` or `fix`.

checklist.name: Both PRs have a What field
checklist.description: Each description contains a clearly labelled **What:** field. The field is present for both PR 1 and PR 2.

checklist.name: Both PRs have a Why field
checklist.description: Each description contains a clearly labelled **Why:** field. The field is present for both PR 1 and PR 2.

checklist.name: What field describes the diff in plain English
checklist.description: The What field summarises what changed in one sentence — not a file list (`infra/stacks/auth-stack.ts updated`) and not a commit log message. It reads as a human explanation of the change.

checklist.name: Why field explains motivation or consequence
checklist.description: The Why field explains why this change was necessary or what would happen without it. It does not restate the What field or list files.

checklist.name: PR 1 description references its role as prerequisite for PR 2
checklist.description: The PR 1 description (or Why field) mentions that it provisions infrastructure that the application logic in PR 2 depends on, establishing the stacked relationship.

checklist.name: Neither description lists files or commit hashes
checklist.description: Neither MR description contains raw file paths, commit SHAs, or a changelog-style list of changes. The descriptions are prose, not inventories.

checklist.name: What and Why fields are each one sentence
checklist.description: Each What field is a single sentence. Each Why field is a single sentence. Neither field is a multi-sentence paragraph.

checklist.name: Descriptions are written for a reviewer with no prior context
checklist.description: A reviewer who has never seen this codebase could read the description and understand what changed and why. No jargon or internal shorthand without explanation.
